skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Coordinates implementation plans by assigning independent tasks to fresh subagents and reviewing each task for specification compliance and code quality.
skill.summary: Run independent implementation tasks through subagents with structured review gates.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution detections are false positives caused by fenced Markdown diagrams and templates in SKILL.md. The reported weak cryptography finding at line 3 is also a false positive; the line contains only the skill description. The skill is safe to publish with a low operational warning because its allowed tools include shell and file modification capabilities for implementation work.

low_findings.title: Broad Implementation Tool Permissions
low_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Edit, Write, and Task permissions. These capabilities are consistent with an implementation orchestration workflow, but they can run commands and modify files during use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration directly includes shell execution and file-writing tools. The surrounding skill content frames them as normal development workflow tools, so this is an operational risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution locations are fenced Markdown blocks containing workflow checklists, diagrams, and review templates. No executable command string or script invocation is present in those blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows only Markdown fences and instructional content. There are no Ruby backticks, shell commands, or dynamic execution patterns at the flagged locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Present
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography issue at line 3 is a false positive. The line is a plain-language description of the skill and does not reference a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected line contains only metadata prose about implementation plans and code review. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related weak cryptographic usage.

content.value_statement: Large implementation plans can lose quality when independent tasks move without review. This skill provides a repeatable subagent workflow with specification checks and code quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to use subagent-driven implementation for independent tasks.
Provides a task dispatch template with context, dependencies, constraints, and acceptance criteria.
Requires a specification compliance review before code quality review.
Describes a revision loop when reviewers find incomplete or incorrect work.
Includes final review steps for integration tests, documentation, and merge readiness.
limitations: Requires a completed implementation plan before use.
Works best when tasks are mostly independent.
Does not provide executable scripts or automation code.
Depends on the host agent and reviewer to enforce each review gate.

use_cases.title: Ship a multi-file feature
use_cases.description: Break a planned feature into independent tasks and review each task before moving forward.

use_cases.title: Coordinate AI coding sessions
use_cases.description: Use a clear dispatch format so each subagent receives context, constraints, and acceptance criteria.

use_cases.title: Improve review discipline
use_cases.description: Apply separate specification and quality reviews before accepting implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Run one planned task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single independent task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for this task. Dispatch one implementer with the task text, relevant files, dependencies, constraints, acceptance criteria, and test requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Execute a task list
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for several independent tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development to execute this implementation plan. Process tasks one at a time, answer clarifying questions, and require spec compliance review before code quality review.

prompt_templates.title: Repair failed review items
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow when a subagent submission does not pass review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for the failed task. Send the issues back to the same implementer, require a revised submission, and repeat both review gates before marking complete.

prompt_templates.title: Complete final integration review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow after all implementation tasks are complete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development final review. Review the full change set, run integration tests, check documentation updates, and confirm merge readiness.

output_examples.input: A developer provides a plan with three independent implementation tasks.
output: Task 1 is dispatched with context and acceptance criteria.
The result passes specification review and code quality review.
The workflow marks Task 1 complete before starting Task 2.

output_examples.input: A reviewer finds missing error handling in a completed task.
output: The task is returned to the same implementer for revision.
The revised work is reviewed again against the original requirements.
The task advances only after both review stages pass.

output_examples.input: All tasks in the plan are marked complete.
output: The workflow performs a final review of the full change set.
Integration tests and documentation status are checked.
The result is prepared for merge when no unresolved issues remain.

best_practices: Start with a complete implementation plan and clear acceptance criteria.
Keep specification review separate from code quality review.
Resolve every reviewer issue before marking a task complete.
anti_patterns: Skipping review gates to save time.
Dispatching vague tasks without relevant files or constraints.
Starting dependent tasks before required predecessor work is accepted.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when you have a completed implementation plan with mostly independent tasks to run in the current session.

faq.question: Does it create subagents automatically?
faq.answer: It provides the workflow and templates for dispatching subagents. Actual execution depends on the host tool.

faq.question: Why are there two review stages?
faq.answer: Specification review checks whether requirements are met. Code quality review checks maintainability, tests, naming, and documentation.

faq.question: Can it handle dependent tasks?
faq.answer: Yes, if dependencies are documented. It works best when most tasks can be handled independently.

faq.question: Does the skill include runnable automation?
faq.answer: No. It is a process guide and set of review templates, not an executable automation package.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found false-positive static detections and one low operational risk from broad development tool permissions.
